objective: rfa CLI binary — read-only commands. Thin REST client targeting topology-ui per D-009. No direct Jaeger access from CLI.
size: small — completed same-day as single chunk
in_scope: rfa mesh node list — GET /api/nodes, table or JSON output
rfa mesh node describe <name> — GET /api/boot-trace?service=<name>, boot span timing table
rfa mesh topology show [--format table|dot|json] — GET /api/nodes + /api/heartbeat per node, DOT graph with peer_count labels
rfa mesh status — GET /api/heartbeat per node, summary table with node_id prefix, peer_count, heartbeat age
rafka.cli.command span per invocation (command, args tags, otel.kind=internal)
rafka.cli.http.request child span per outbound HTTP call (method, path, otel.kind=client)
400ms sleep before process exit ensures BatchSpanProcessor flushes to Jaeger
GET /api/heartbeat?service=X added to topology-ui — proxies Jaeger rafka.mesh.heartbeat query, returns node_id + peer_count + last_heartbeat_us
out_of_scope: Direct Jaeger access from CLI (D-009: CLI talks only to topology-ui)
exit_criteria: rfa mesh node list returns 4 nodes — DONE (registry, compute, gateway, broker)
rfa mesh node describe gateway returns node_id + 5 boot span timings — DONE
rfa mesh topology show --format dot produces valid DOT with 4 nodes + peer_count labels — DONE (3 peers each)
rfa mesh status prints 4-row table with node_id + peer_count + heartbeat age — DONE
rafka.cli.command spans visible in Jaeger — DONE (6 traces total, 4 command spans with correct tags)
rafka.cli.http.request child spans in same traces — DONE
note: DOT topology show is nodes-only — peer identity (edges) not available until iroh-gossip peer tracking lands (sprint-10+). Honest gap flagged in DOT comment.
